goal: Fix two Related Links bugs on the reading result: (1) the 'Related Internal Links' heading overlapping the card link below it; (2) reversed-card thumbnails shown as a rotated-180 upright image, which looks ridiculous in the small thumbnail.
root_cause: (1) reading.js built the panel title as a <header> element; a global, over-broad `header { position: fixed }` rule in styles.css (meant for #site-header) leaked onto it, pinning the title out of normal flow so the first .related-links__hero slid up underneath (measured 16px overlap, head computed position:fixed). (2) reading.js loaded the UPRIGHT art and added .is-reversed -> CSS rotate(180deg) for reversed cards in 'flip' mode.
fix: (1) Changed the related-links title element from <header> to <div> (same .related-links__head class/CSS) so the global header rule no longer matches it -> title back in flow, 18px gap restored, no overlap (verified live by forcing position:static: gap 18px, stillOverlaps false). (2) For reversed cards in the related-links thumb, load the dedicated reversed art via buildCardImageUrls(card,'reversed') -> candidates [reversedUrl, uprightUrl, backUrl] and drop the .is-reversed rotate class; degrades to un-rotated upright then back if reversed art is missing. Both changes are in reading.js; bumped the shared cache-bust ?v=20260625a->20260625b site-wide so the versioned reading.js delivers; mirrored to www/.
verified: reading.js node --check OK; live DOM: head was position:fixed overlapping hero by 16px, and forcing it static restored an 18px gap with no overlap. Reversed-art path uses the same resolver the app already uses for card faces (backUrl always present as final fallback). Post-deploy: confirm on a reading result with a reversed related-link card.
